purpose: Resolves the apparent triple cross-asset inconsistency (SPX 21x, rates pricing 50-75bp cuts, DXY 103-106) by decomposing the ~75-100bp FCI-policy rate gap into its component sources.
components: Asymmetric tightening speed: FCI eases over months but tightens in days (~75-100bp instantaneously)
Resolution: equities price loose FCI, rates price Fed convergence to those conditions, dollar prices non-rate capital flow factors
current_assessment: Four agents independently validated the same magnitudes from different analytical frames. The decomposition is near-consensus as an explanatory framework. Key risk is the asymmetric tightening speed — if equities or credit correct, FCI can tighten by the equivalent of 3-4 simultaneous rate hikes in days.
limitations: Component magnitudes may shift as market conditions change
Does not predict when FCI reconvergence will occur
Equity multiple expansion (not earnings growth) driving FCI loosening creates reflexivity risk
Assumes FCI components are roughly additive; interaction effects may be significant
